verses.1: Afterward Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.
verses.3: “May the day on which I was to be born perish, As well as the night which said, ‘A boy is conceived.’
verses.4: “May that day be darkness; May God above not care for it, Nor light shine on it.
verses.5: “May darkness and black gloom claim it; May a cloud settle on it; May the blackness of the day terrify it.
verses.6: “ As for that night, may darkness seize it; May it not rejoice among the days of the year; May it not come into the number of the months.
verses.7: “Behold, may that night be barren; May no joyful shout enter it.
verses.8: “May those curse it who curse the day, Who are prepared to disturb Leviathan.
verses.9: “May the stars of its twilight be darkened; May it wait for light but have none, And may it not see the breaking dawn;
verses.10: Because it did not shut the opening of my mother’s womb, Or hide trouble from my eyes.
verses.11: “Why did I not die at birth, Come out of the womb and pass away?
verses.12: “Why were the knees there in front of me, And why the breasts, that I would nurse?
verses.13: “For now I would have lain down and been quiet; I would have slept then, I would have been at rest,
verses.14: With kings and counselors of the earth, Who rebuilt ruins for themselves;
verses.15: Or with rulers who had gold, Who were filling their houses with silver.
verses.16: “Or like a miscarriage which is hidden, I would not exist, As infants that never saw light.
verses.17: “There the wicked cease from raging, And there the weary are at rest.
verses.18: “The prisoners are at ease together; They do not hear the voice of the taskmaster.
verses.19: “The small and the great are there, And the slave is free from his master.
verses.20: “Why is light given to one burdened with grief, And life to the bitter of soul,
verses.21: Who long for death, but there is none, And dig for it more than for hidden treasures;
verses.22: Who are filled with jubilation, And rejoice when they find the grave?
verses.23: “ Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden, And whom God has shut off?
verses.24: “For my groaning comes at the sight of my food, And my cries pour out like water.
verses.25: “For what I fear comes upon me, And what I dread encounters me.
verses.26: “I am not at ease, nor am I quiet, And I am not at rest, but turmoil comes.”
